note: Right-aware SDXL inpaint API workflow. Raw VAEDecode output is saved; final soft composite is done by scripts/soft-composite-restore.py.
prompt_text:
﻿Pair-specific panoramic adapter for dawn-valley to dusk-ridge.
Use the left anchor as the actual right edge of dawn-valley and the right anchor as the actual left edge of dusk-ridge.

Generate a wide natural transition world between them.
Left side: preserve the dawn valley feeling, warm low sunlight, open air, low contrast mist.
Middle: gradually reduce contrast, sink into fog, recede into layered distance, no dominant central peak, no dark wall.
Right third: deliberately turn cooler before reaching the right anchor. Bring in blue-violet dusk atmosphere, cool mist, distant layered ridges, and subdued rocky terrain that naturally continues into the dusk-ridge plate.

The color temperature must shift smoothly from warm dawn on the left to cool blue-violet dusk on the right.
Avoid a warm sunlit mountain near the right edge. Avoid a vertical warm wall. Avoid a central dark mountain mass. Avoid a hard vertical color band. Avoid pasted split-screen composition.
Preserve the anchor crops where the mask is black and regenerate the white center band plus slight overmask area.
The result should feel like one continuous panoramic journey, not two wallpapers taped together.
negative_prompt_text: large black mountain on the left edge, hard structure jump, abrupt horizon step, lake hitting mountain, pasted collage, split-screen image, vertical seam line, transparent area, empty blank center, sharp black silhouette at dawn-valley boundary, mismatched perspective, text, watermark, people, buildings, vehicles
